item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Image could not be sourced reliably and is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: REFRUITOLUTION!!!!!!!!!!!! +1, it is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good this stream of fruit puns is over. They where getting rotten anyways.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is interesting, but it feels a little forced, maybe vague is a better word actually, for my tastes.

A fruit rights activist cut into three different selves, pretty neat to say the least, but there are hints that it has some kind of influence on fruit it converses with, yeah? Did it give a researcer that ate said banana some kind of [REDACTED]?

Very well done, even if I think it's full capabilities aren't expressed as much as they should be. Personal preferences aside, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if all grapefruits are sapient, but we are oppressing them, and this is the only one that wants to stand up.

We must contain him, before he starts World Core I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
anomalous citrus
godbless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Orange power!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This whole conversation has been so ban(an)al.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the Che's-last-words reference, but this definitely feels too silly for a mainlister, and I have a high tolerance for silliness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy crap.
Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. Especially how it seems so ridiculously silly, but what if… what if all fruits are sapient, and this one is anomalous because we can tell?
Dun dun DUNNNNNNNNN!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had to downvote this. It's just… really dumb. I don't know how else to describe it. It's a grapefruit with the personalities of revolutionaries, and makes speeches about freeing fruits from their shackles. In what way is this not a J?
I feel like the writing and style of the article are well done, so kudos to the author for that, but the core concept of this SCP is so fundamentally, well, dumb, that I can't see any amount of revision making it worthwhile, although I seem to be in the minority on that opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me a bit of "The Views of an Erewhonian Philosopher Concerning the Rights of Vegetables" from Erewhon. Except from the perspective of the plant itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am very glad that I am reading the version with original speeches. I salute your audacity, author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is how you do a funny mainlist SCP.
Ave Fructis!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, done. You were definitely right, this feels better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, i get what you're saying. You're definitely right. I'll change it. I just need a name for "The Jewish Barber". Xenophobic dictator won't work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe go into detail on what kinds of literature or audio it has requested, and how it reacts to it. That information kind of goes nowhere in the article.
Additionally, has the orange begun to decay? Is it ripe in perpetuity? These would help flesh it out I think. While entertaining, it's not totally set apart from other talking things that shouldn't be able to talk, in my opinion, like the talking spider or 329-J, the ghost sign. Not upvoting, but not downvoting.

Also, ██/██/201█? You could really only put a 0, 1, or 2, so it's not hiding that much information. I don't think it adds anything to redact the year. Really not sure why the date has to be redacted at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, the persona IS NOT MLK. It might think it's MLK, but it is not MLK, and the Foundation WOULD NOT CALL IT THAT.

Activist Preacher, Xenophobic Dictator, and Latin American Revolutionary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who deals out bad puns like there's no tomato tomorrow, I always feel threatened by that article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We do have a history of silly, mainlist fruit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, it's sapient.

Also, imagine if your fucking grapefruit just started inspirational speexhing at you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came for the SCP and stayed for the inevitable Discussion puns.

You did a good job. I didn't really think from a first glance that I would like this, but you did a good job at presenting a surreal and silly idea seriously, and you connected the elements of the idea together in a way that made sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
John Dies at the End. Read it.

Horror and humor do not have to be separate, and actually work excellently together. See, this is bizarre. It doesn't make sense. It's unsettling. That makes it work better in the SCP universe than the umpteenth spooky building or predator with big scary teeth. Those are mundane. You know what's coming.

This, this doesn't play by the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like a lot of what is going on here; the sentient fruit that believes it's various dictators and activists, but this one is just way too silly for me to accept it as a non-J SCP. Breakfasts planning uprisings, MLK speeches altered to be about fruit, it just seems far too humorous to be consistent with everything else in the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
her breakfast was planning an uprising

This pretty much sold me on the SCP.

It's silly, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is zany, and the article is filled with fruit pun-filled speeches derived from some famous speeches. But I didn't upvote it cause it I thought the puns were utterly hilarious or anything. I upvoted because when you strip that away, this isn't just a talking fruit.

I liked the article because it's about a fruit that's emulating personalities of figures like MLK and Che Guevara while (or because, or the personalities are causing it to) "trying" to revolt against humans. It has conversations with other fruit regarding this agenda. This scenario is what appeals to me. It just so happens unfortunately for the grapefruit that it can't move or do anything except talk.

And influence…

The only thing that does bother me about the article is (although it originally intrigued me I admit) the psychological diagnosis. What on earth would lead a researcher to presume something like this; it's not like it's human or anything. The situations is so out there, I don't think DID would be the first thing to come to mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Such hostility! Kiwi all just get along?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I reckon that's a plum good idea, Hurtz.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: claiming that her breakfast was planning an uprising. Upvoted just for that. +1 for you good si— GRAPEFRUIT

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We should probably back-petal and get to the pit of the issue before Bright rinds us to a pulp.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All these fruit puns are unpearable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really think Lord Blackwood does the whole "sapient X that isn't supposed to be sapient" angle much better than this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of like this, but not as an SCP, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I can see what you're saying about naming the personas. Earlier today, I did change the speeches to be original.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… think I can see ways to make this better.

Don't call the personas "Martin Luther King Jr" and "the Jewish Barber from the Great Dictator". Call them "Activist Preacher" and "Xenophobic Dictator". And give them better damn speeches that aren't just their actual speeches with fruit puns added in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just too silly, and it's not even that entertaining to me. Sorry, but it's a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so horrendously, hilariously stupid that I should not have upvoted, but I did anyway. Enjoy it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Way, waaaay too silly for my tastes. Also some cheapening of a fairly important historical moment to boot.

Downvoted. I have no idea why this is rated as high as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha! Fruit! Get it?! Because it's a talking fruit with zany little fruit jokes! It's weird and quirky!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a problem partly because plagiarism, but mostly because it turned existing speeches into fruit jokes. Hence why it felt like a -J. With the speeches changed, that is no longer as great an issue. It remains a bit silly, but not the "LOL GUISE" kind of way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvote rescinded.

No vote for now. I will come back to this someday and see how I feel about it after some time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: POMEGRANITE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, I reworked the speech from 1794-B. working on -A now
EDIT: Both entries are now replaced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a talking fruit. So? I'm not following the current with this one and up-voting just because this is odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just a bunch of famous speeches with fruit jokes in them. And not even clever jokes at that. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The problem of the speeches is that it seems plagiar-ish. You prooobably shouldn't have taken them word for word and just put fruit puns in. However, this could be easily solved if you just rewrote a new fruit speech that just sounded like something those people would say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this really a big deal? That's the approach I chose to take. It may have bee a bit lazienss, but I think mainly that's just the best way I could think of to perserve the personality. If enough people tell me that this is a problem, I'll remove the article. This is just the way I imagined it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's definitely very well written but as an idea it just seems like zany for zaniness' sake. Other people seem to be enjoying it though, so I'll keep to a neutral vote on this one, because perhaps I'm just being grumpy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After re-reading and finding that you didn't make funny new versions of the speeches, but just used fruit puns, I switched my upvote to a downvote. That's a really lame thing to do man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THIS IS EXTREMELY SILLY.

I like it. But I have a question- is "prior to an interview" necessary in the image description? It left me with the impression that it would change when it was talking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just too cute, too cute by half. I don't like that it's so heavily dependent on movie quotes and archetype humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to agree with this viewpoint. There's not a lot of substance here, really, it's just a grapefruit what can think and tends to recite speeches composed entirely of fruit puns. Also, "Chew, you are only going to eat a fruit" actually made me groan a little inside, and I like terrible puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, leaf him alone, not all puns are intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me, it got to the "No, not really" part when I read the speech from 'The Great Dictator' with a more fruity variation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, branches out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And just when I thought the talking animals craze was over, it instead branches out .__.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A carrot is a vegetable! Sorry, but little mistakes like that make me go bananas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I started reading I was like "oh crap. This is a really creative idea, and very daring, because this is totally a -j idea being done on the mainlist. Let's see how it turned out."

And then I read the Martin Luther King Jr. speech and was very disappointed to find that all you did was take his existing speech and alter it to be about fruit. That's not clever, original, or interesting, and that IS a lame joke that has no place in a mainlist article. This is juvenile. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good, but "Chew, you are only going to eat a fruit." Is what really makes it for me. +1
